channel.title: Comments for page "Ambrose London Prix Fixe"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Ambrose London Prix Fixe"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well this is an unexpected Christmas gift.

A more high class restaurant than the standard Ambrose fair, and I'll be damned if you didn't pull it off. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was all set to upvote this - there's a healthy variety to the menu items that keeps it lively, and the first two reviews are pretty authentic.

Then I got to the final review. The crosslink, I figured, was a nice nod. I didn't even need to click it to know the reference. This fell apart for me there though, because it's not content to just reference the Joke article, but it has to give a beat-for-beat rendition of it. Only a lot less funny.

Starts off creatively, then takes a nosedive in originality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great, now I'm hungry.

But not for crab-stuffed mushroom.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"While the Baked Potato Bug calls to mind a different kind of establishment - one that caters to a somewhat lower clientele, I found it to be excellently prepared, and that the exotic origin of the bug made up for the base subject matter. (Unnaturally generated along with conceptual potato based matter, spawned from what may be the conceptual ideal of a potato. As such, this was the best execution of the dish possible.)"

This line is so wonderfully up its own ass. +1~!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: THIS fully lives up to the posh headcanon I had for Ambrose. Tempting, bizarre foods sourced from around the wiki, fancy-schmantzy writing, and humorously mixed reviews from anomalous-food critics. Outstanding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to say that I LOVE this!!! You did some great work, Storm! THANK YOU for this excellent present!!!
